stories.title: An Iranian-American writer describes the "vertigo" he feels about the war
stories.description: Host Adrian Ma speaks with Iranian American writer Nick Mafi about the war in Iran. Mafi says many Iranians in the US are feeling a sense of vertigo because of the conflict.

stories.title: 'We never asked for a ceasefire,' says Iran's foreign minister, as war keeps raging
stories.description: Israel announced a barrage of new strikes on western Iran on Sunday, while Iran's foreign minister said the country has not asked for a ceasefire as President Trump had claimed.

stories.title: Russian strike on Kyiv region kills 4 and wounds 15, with peace talks stalled
stories.description: The strikes comes after the United States paused ceasefire talks between Russia and Ukraine due to the war with Iran.

stories.title: How the conflict in Iran is affecting global markets
stories.description: Escalation of the Iran conflict is sending shockwaves through global markets — driving up oil, fuel, and commodity prices, stoking inflation and recession risks worldwide. We hear from three NPR reporters in Europe, Asia and Russia.

stories.title: U.S. Embassy in Baghdad again urges Americans to leave Iraq as Trump touts strikes on Iran
stories.description: In a post on Truth Social, President Trump claimed the U.S. had "destroyed 100% of Iran's Military capability." The U.S. Embassy in Baghdad, meanwhile, urged Americans to "leave Iraq immediately."

stories.title: These are the casualties and cost of the war in Iran 2 weeks into the conflict
stories.description: The war in Iran has already cost the U.S. billions of dollars. Here's the impact by the numbers.

stories.title: Iran captain latest footballer to drop asylum bid, Iranian state media says
stories.description: Five of the seven members of the women's football delegation who originally sought to stay in Australia have now chosen to return home.

stories.title: US names six crew killed in refuelling plane crash in Iraq
stories.description: The aircraft was on a combat mission as part of ongoing US operations against Iran when it crashed in western Iraq on Thursday.

stories.title: Under drone fire, exiled Kurds wait to confront Iranian regime
stories.description: A group of fighters based in Iraq say they are "ready to go home", as they face drone attacks from Tehran.

stories.title: Why has the US targeted Iran's Kharg Island?
stories.description: The tiny island is home to one of the most critical pieces of Iran's energy infrastructure.

stories.title: Hamas urges key ally Iran to halt attacks on Gulf states
stories.description: The Palestinian group also affirms Tehran's right to defend itself from "aggression" by the US and Israel.

stories.title: Iranians react to new supreme leader's first address
stories.description: People in Iran are questioning who's really in charge after Mojtaba Khamenei's first address came via a presenter on state TV.

stories.title: 'There's no hiding place on a ship': The sailors stranded near Iran
stories.description: Drones, cruise missiles and fighter jets have become a common sight for many sailors stranded on ships in the Gulf.

stories.title: Bodies of 84 Iranian sailors killed in US torpedo strike to be repatriated
stories.description: The seamen died when Iranian warship the Iris Dena was sunk on 4 March by a US submarine near Sri Lanka.

stories.title: Emirati minister tells BBC Iran must end strikes on Gulf
stories.description: The UAE says more than 1,800 drones and missiles have been projected at the country since the war began.

stories.title: 'Fingers on the trigger': Deadly warnings for Iranians being urged to take action
stories.description: Iran's police chief has warned that his forces would treat anyone taking to the streets "at the enemy's request" as an enemy.

stories.title: Why did US and Israel attack Iran and how long could the war last?
stories.description: Conflict has spread across the Middle East since the US and Israel launched wide-ranging strikes on Iran on 28 February.

stories.title: How Iranians are evading internet blocks to contact family abroad
stories.description: Iranians are finding tech-savvy ways to get through the regime's restrictions on phone and internet connections.

stories.title: As hopes of regime change in Iran fade, Netanyahu faces political test
stories.description: Israeli leaders are framing the bombing campaign as having transformed the Middle East in their favour as pressure to end the conflict builds.

stories.title: Mission accomplished? The 2003 boast that haunts today's Iran conflict
stories.description: The echoes between the conflicts are certainly there but there are also profound differences.

stories.title: Tracking the wave of ship attacks that has choked off Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Six ships have been reportedly attacked in the Gulf in less than 48 hours, bringing the total vessels attacked during the war to 18.

stories.title: Being an artist and a refugee: Amir Amiri and Parisa Karimi Molan speak to France 24
stories.description: France 24’s Gavin Lee speaks with Amir Amiri and Parisa Karimi, two Iranian musicians who now live in canada. They discuss the difficulties and hopes of being refugees and artists from a war-torn country.

stories.title: Turnout low in French mayoral elections seen as key test ahead of 2027 presidential race
stories.description: French voters cast ballots in municipal elections marred by low turnout on Sunday, in the last major test of the public mood ahead of next year’s all-important presidential election. The run-up to the vote was largely overshadowed by the Iran war and its fallout, notably the impa...

stories.title: From missiles strikes to rocketing oil price: Guy Laron speaks to France 24 to analyse the economic consequences of Iran war
stories.description: France 24’s Gavin Lee speaks with Guy Laron, Senior lecturer in international relations, the Hebrew University of Jerusalem, about the economic consequences of the ongoing war in Iran, from rising oil prices to how the strategic Strait of Hormuz can be used as an economic weapon.

stories.title: Trump administration threatens media outlets over Iran war coverage
stories.description: The head of the Federal Communications Commission, which oversees US media outlets, has warned that some broadcasters risk losing their licenses over their news coverage of the Iran war. Brendan Carr – a Trump appointee – wrote in a social media post that broadcasters must operat...

stories.title: US and Israel carry out new strikes on western Iran
stories.description: Israel says it's carrying out fresh strikes on western Iran - as the war enters its third week. Israeli officials say the bombing campaign is wide-scale and targeting regime infrastructure. This comes as the IDF says it has detected missiles fired from Iran. Details by Hoda Abdel...

stories.title: The humanitarian crisis worsens in Lebanon, with over 850,000 displaced
stories.description: In just 10 days, more than 800,000 people in Lebanon have been displaced by war, just over a year since the last conflict uprooted over a million Lebanese from their homes. Israeli strikes have killed 826 people, including 106 children and 65 women, since the conflict between Hez...

stories.title: Are Iran's retaliatory strikes across the Gulf region affecting the US' war strategy?
stories.description: Israel's military said it began a broad wave of strikes on western Iran on Sunday. As the war progresses into the third week, further retaliatory strikes from Iran are likely to occur. So far, Iran has launched strikes across nine countries in the Gulf region. Most of these attac...

stories.title: Trump urges allies to send warships to Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: President Donald Trump has threatened to get the Strait of Hormuz opened "one way or another" and has called on a number of countries including France, the United Kingdom and China to send ships to the area to overcome Iran's blockade on the vital trade artery. It comes as global...

stories.title: Week in pictures: Trouble in the Strait of Hormuz, Iran’s new leader and a Trump-Epstein statue
stories.description: From deadly strikes in Iran and Lebanon, to trouble in the Strait of Hormuz and a statue of US President Donald Trump with sex offender Jeffrey Epstein in a pose from the movie "Titanic", FRANCE 24 takes a look at some of the week's most striking images.

stories.title: Who is running Iran? The regime's new strongmen
stories.description: In the third week of war, who is leading the country? And to what extent have those still in place been destabilised and weakened by the US and Israeli strikes on the country? A veil of mystery surrounds a man who remains out of sight: Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei. ...

stories.title: What role is oil playing in the war in the Middle East and what's at stake?
stories.description: More than two weeks ago, the United States and Israel launched attacks on Iran. Since then, Iran has retaliated against targets across the Gulf and effectively halted shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. Global oil prices have surged as a result. Did the United States factor th...

stories.title: Ukraine recovering from Russian strike, concerned Putin is benefiting from Iran war
stories.description: Ukraine is recovering from a wave of Russian strikes that killed six people and wounded dozens across multiple regions on Saturday. Authorities say residential buildings, educational institutions, businesses and critical infrastructure were hit – particularly in Kyiv where about ...

stories.title: Qatar, Kuwait, UAE... Iranian reprisals disrupt daily life across the Gulf
stories.description: Missiles intercepted in Qatar; two drones causing damage at an airbase near a major US military site in Kuwait; an oil facility hit and ports threatened in the UAE... Gulf countries continue to pay the price of war every day, more than two weeks after the start of Operation Epic ...

stories.title: Iran’s ambassador to Saudi Arabia denies attacks on its oil facilities
stories.description: Alireza Enayati says relations with Saudi Arabia are 'progressing naturally' and he's in contact with Saudi officials.

stories.title: A dozen arrests as hundreds attend Al-Quds Day rally in London
stories.description: Police make 12 arrests as demonstrators defy government restrictions to gather on Thames embankment.

stories.title: Ukraine eyes money and tech in return for Middle East drone support
stories.description: Ukraine leader previously said advisers were sent to Qatar, UAE, and Saudi Arabia to help thwart Iranian drone attacks.

stories.title: US Senator Van Hollen: Trump ‘has no idea where this war is going’
stories.description: Democratic US Senator Chris Van Hollen argues that US-Israel war on Iran ‘is making America and our allies less safe'.

stories.title: Strategic oil release may calm markets but cannot fix Hormuz disruption
stories.description: Record oil release steadies markets, but war-driven disruption in the Strait of Hormuz keeps energy supplies uncertain.

stories.title: Trump calls for naval coalition to open Strait of Hormuz: Can it work?
stories.description: Analysts say US president's solution to secure the strait is fraught with problems.

stories.title: Moment Iranian missile hits Tel Aviv
stories.description: CCTV footage released by Israeli police shows the moment an Iranian missile struck a street in Tel Aviv.

stories.title: Captain of Iran’s women’s team withdraws Australia asylum bid: State media
stories.description: Zahra Ghanbari is fifth football player to withdraw her request after seven squad members sought asylum in Australia.

stories.title: What do the first two weeks of the war on Iran portend for the future?
stories.description: New, critical dynamics are emerging in the region that could reshape it in dramatic ways.

stories.title: Keisuke Honda loses US advertising deal over Iran support at World Cup
stories.description: Japanese football legend says his opinion prompts US company to cancel advertising campaign before FIFA World Cup.

stories.title: ICC judge says US sanctions left her without bank cards and Google accounts
stories.description: An ICC judge says sanctions imposed by the Trump administration are punishing her for carrying out her judicial duties.

stories.title: Tehran reminds Modi about need to activate BRICS
stories.description: • Under India’s chairmanship, the expanded bloc has remained silent • Pezeshkian, Araghchi urge Indian counterparts to spearhead a ‘strong and constructive’ intervention • New Delhi caught in a diplomatic bind amid domestic LPG crisis; balances pro-Israel comments with its ‘frien...

stories.title: ‘I am dead … for coffee’: Israeli PM Netanyahu posts his video amid speculation about his death
stories.description: A video posted on Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s X account is being seen as his response to the rumours of his death, which were earlier debunked by his office. The video was posted on Sunday evening, in which Netanyahu is seen getting himself a coffee and being aske...

stories.title: US assures of ‘reliable’ energy supply to Asia-Pacific
stories.description: TOKYO: The United States can provide “reliable” energy supplies to Asia-Pacific, US Interior Secretary Doug Burgum said on Saturday, as the Middle East war cripples the region’s oil and gas flow. Oil and gas prices have surged since US-Israeli strikes on Iran killed its supreme l...

stories.title: Israeli bombing claims six lives in Gaza
stories.description: Smoke rises as the Israeli army demolishes a Palestinian house in Dura, a town in the Israeli-occupied West Bank.—Reuters GAZA CITY: Israeli strikes killed six people in Gaza Strip on Saturday, according to the territory’s civil defence agency. In a pre-dawn strike, two policemen...

stories.title: Five killed in Russian air attacks on Ukraine
stories.description: KYIV: Russia hammered Ukraine with missiles and drones on Saturday, killing five people and causing damage across several regions of the country, Ukrainian officials said. The main target was energy infrastructure outside the capital Kyiv, President Volodymyr Zelensky said, addin...

stories.title: War escalates as US strikes Iran’s energy lifeline
stories.description: • US bombs Kharg Island, key to 90pc of oil exports• Tehran warns it will target any facility in region linked to US• Trump says warships from several countries to guard Hormuz• US embassy in Baghdad urges Americans to leave Iraq• Iran Guards say targeted US forces at Saudi base ...

stories.title: Israeli strike kills 12 Lebanon medics amid peace push
stories.description: A SITE in the Haret Hreik, a neighbourhood in southern Beirut which was struck by Israel.—AFP • Death toll for paramedics this month rises to 31 after clinic bombing• France offers to host negotiations in Paris; urges Israel to halt its offensive• First direct talks since conflic...

stories.title: Trump administration slams CNN over Iran war coverage
stories.description: • Pete Hegseth calls reporting ‘patently ridiculous’ • Thompson says network will keep reporting ‘the truth’ WASHINGTON: The Pentagon and White House slammed CNN over its Iran war coverage on Friday — with Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth saying he looked forward to an ally of Pres...

stories.title: War Diary Day 16: Trump losing initiative as allies reluctant, Iran defiant
stories.description: On the sixteenth day of the US–Israel war on Iran, the conflict continued to rage across multiple theatres as Tehran and its regional allies sustained pressure through missile strikes, proxy attacks and maritime disruption. Meanwhile, the US and Israeli campaign showed limited pr...

stories.title: Iran holds the key to reopening global energy markets
stories.description: When Saudi Aramco told its oil buyers in a letter this week that it had no clear idea which port it would use for April exports, it laid bare a new reality: Iran, not the United States, holds the key to reopening the global energy market. The letter, sent to Saudi oil buyers arou...

stories.title: Hamas urges Iran to spare neighbours from bombings
stories.description: • Palestinian group affirms Tehran’s right to retaliate against ‘Zionist aggression’ • Calls for immediate international action to halt wider conflict GAZA CITY: The Palestinian re­­sistance movement Hamas on Sat­urday called on its ally Iran to avoid targeting neighbouring count...

stories.title: Trump claims ‘many countries’ will send warships to keep Strait of Hormuz open
stories.description: US President Donald Trump said on Saturday that many countries would send warships to keep the Strait of Hormuz open, but did not provide details on which countries would do so. “Many countries, especially those who are affected by Iran’s attempted closure of the Hormuz Strait, w...

stories.title: Iran’s uranium enrichment secures independence, peaceful atom — supreme leader
stories.description: Ali Khamenei added that the US and other countries are not interested in Iran's progress and have put forward conditions presupposing a complete halt of uranium enrichment on Iranian territory

stories.title: Tehran could weigh nuclear deal with US with Iran-based enrichment consortium — Axios
stories.description: According to the report, a sixth round of talks on the Iranian nuclear program between the US president’s Special Envoy Steve Witkoff and Iran’s top diplomat, Abbas Araghchi, could be held this weekend in a Middle Eastern country

stories.title: DPRK slams U.S. for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang
stories.description: The Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) has slammed the United States for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang.
